text.title: LLM Observability and Monitoring — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of `llm-observability` must enforce. Verifiable at review time; failures gate the artifact.

rule.statement: Every LLM call is traced with input + output + tokens_in + tokens_out + latency + cost_usd.
rule.rationale: Untraced calls are invisible to cost + quality + post-mortem analysis.

rule.statement: Run held-out eval probes on a schedule (≥daily); alert on metric drop.
rule.rationale: Without continuous eval, regressions land silently between releases.

rule.statement: Alert when daily cost > 2x 7-day moving average OR when single-call cost > 100x median.
rule.rationale: Cost spikes signal retry storms / runaway loops / scraping.

rule.statement: Redact PII before sending traces to vendor; never trust vendor-side redaction alone.
rule.rationale: Vendor-side redaction varies; client-side is auditable.

rule.statement: 100% trace for first N days; sample to <100% only when volume forces it; never sample evals.
rule.rationale: Sampling hides rare bugs; full coverage initially is cheap insurance.

rule.statement: Pick vendor matching data-residency policy; default Langfuse self-host for EU customer data.
rule.rationale: Cross-region trace storage may breach GDPR or contract terms.
